Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend The Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SONJA DAHL, TEL. NO. 1-925-287-8181 APPEARING FOR THE DEFEDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF TRACY BUCKLEY'S Motion To Amend The Complaint to allege punitive damages against health care providers is Denied. Ms. Buckley has not satisfied her burden of showing that she possesses a legally sufficient claim for punitive damages supported by admissible evidence. (See College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704.) The evidence submitted by Ms. Buckley indicates that defendants Dr. Christopher Scott and Dr. Holly Wilson's treatment of Ms. Buckley amounted to medical negligence or gross negligence. But proof of negligence, recklessness, or gross negligence is insufficient to warrant an award of punitive damages. (Dawes v. Superior Court (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 82, 87.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

There being no objections made by any party, the Court approves the language of parties' proposed order. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications to conform with the language of parties' proposed order. As to DEFENDANT PARAMJIT GILL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, Dr. Gill's demurrer to the first, fourth and fifth causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained as to all three claims with twenty days leave to amend. The first and fourth claims do not adequately allege the conduct of Dr. Gill that plaintiff contends constitutes medical negligence and false imprisonment. Once the demurrer to the first and fourth claims are sustained, there is no predicate conduct attributable to Dr. Gill for which he is alleged to have done in a racially discriminatory manner and thus the fifth claim is rendered inadequate as well. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, Dignity Health's demurrer to the third and fifth causes of action is overruled as to both claims. Both claims are adequately pled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a violation of Health and Safety Code 1317(a) by her allegations that she was not given a screening or the screening she received was so cursory as to be of no help to her, and that she was suffering from a serious injury or illness. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a violation of Health and Safety Code 1317(b) and the Unruh Act by her allegations that she received poor emergency services medical care due to her race. ORDER SIGNED.
